attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who wins from reduced groups rather than solo efforts, typically timing final sprint in last 200m from small selections. Prefers controlled breakaway sprints to long-range attacks.
team_tactics: Minimal team support visible; relies on opponent teams exhausting themselves in chase (UAE, Movistar examples). Benefits from chaotic race situations and peloton disorganization rather than controlled lead-outs.
terrain_preference: Uphill sprint finishes and rolling terrain where reduced groups form naturally. Exposed on sustained climbs above 7% gradient where weight becomes limiting factor. Excels in breakaway scenarios on flat-to-rolling stages.
vulnerability: Sustained climbing above 7% grade exposes weight/power limitations (Paris-Nice 7.1% evidence). Lacks explosive punch for pure punchy finishes when fresh contenders present (Bosberg). Needs disorganized peloton or exhausted opponents.
key_patterns: Wins from small breakaway groups (4-riders or reduced selections) not solo efforts: 4 instances
Exceptional sprint timing in final 200m from slipstream position, repeated execution across years
Thrives when weather chaos or team exhaustion disrupts organized chase: 3 documented cases
Mental resilience: won day after losing in final meters, repeated KOM efforts show persistence
Early breakaway participant collecting KOM points when peloton permits: 6 consecutive wins documented
form_trajectory: neutral: Mixed 2023 signals—won from chaos/breakaway (consistent pattern) but yellow jersey exposed climbing weakness at 7%. Weight/climbing trade-off unchanged from previous seasons.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
